channel.title: The Signal — Market Intelligence
channel.description: AI, defense, space & cyber stock analysis

item.title: Rocket Lab Just Became the Most Important Space Stock You Don't Own
item.description: Rocket Lab just hit 87 Electron launches, deployed 250+ satellites, and is building Neutron — a reusable medium-lift rocket. With end-to-end spacecraft manufacturing, growing government contracts, and a fraction of SpaceX's valuation, RKLB is the space stock that actually makes sense.

item.title: Marvell Is the AI Semiconductor Giant Wall Street Is Ignoring
item.description: Marvell hit $189 after a 263% run from its 52-week low, posting $8.2B revenue and a 32.6% profit margin. With custom AI silicon exploding and optical interconnect demand surging, MRVL is the most underfollowed pure AI infrastructure play in semiconductors.

item.title: CoreWeave Is the AI Cloud Dark Horse Nobody's Talking About
item.description: CoreWeave (CRWV) runs $6.2B in annual revenue as the premier GPU-as-a-service cloud for AI workloads. Up 50% YTD with a $138 analyst target, this is the infrastructure bet that captures AI compute demand without guessing which model wins.

item.title: RTX Just Dipped 18% From Its Highs. Here's Why That's Your Entry Point.
item.description: RTX dropped 18% from its $214 high despite posting $90B revenue, $7.2B FCF, and a growing dividend. At 25x forward earnings with a 28% analyst upside and defense budgets only expanding, this is the kind of dip you buy, not fear.

item.title: Why AXON is the Ultimate Unkillable Portfolio Diversifier
item.description: Axon Enterprise (AXON) crossed $1.5B in ARR with 125% NRR and AI revenue exploding 700% YoY. At $386 — nearly 50% off highs — this defense-style AI monopoly is the ultimate asymmetric portfolio hedge.

item.title: SpaceX IPO: Everything Investors Need to Know Before the Biggest Space Debut in History
item.description: SpaceX is preparing what could be the largest IPO in history with a potential $300B+ valuation. With Starlink's cash flow accelerating and Starship achieving orbit, the bull case is compelling — but history says the best entry may come months after the debut.

item.title: Meta Is Spending $65B on AI in 2026 — Smart Bet or Billionaire's Gamble?
item.description: Meta's $65B AI capex plan has investors divided. With Llama 4 open-source AI gaining traction, Advantage+ ad revenue accelerating, and the metaverse pivot fading into the background, the bull case is stronger than ever — but the spending has no modern precedent.

item.title: Broadcom's AI Networking Dominance: Tomahawk, Custom ASICs, and the VMware Pivot
item.description: Broadcom's Tomahawk 5 switches connect Nvidia GPUs in every major hyperscaler cluster. Custom ASIC deals with Google, Meta, and Apple add $10B+ in AI revenue. VMware is pivoting to subscription, and the dividend just grew 18% — making AVGO a must-own in AI infrastructure.

item.title: AMD vs Nvidia in AI: Is 2026 Finally AMD's Year to Compete?
item.description: AMD's Instinct MI350 is winning real hyperscaler deployments, and MI400 is on track for 2027. But Nvidia's Blackwell ramp and CUDA ecosystem remain formidable. We analyze whether AMD can capture 20%+ of the AI GPU market.

item.title: SpaceX: Inside the Hidden Debt and Hype Behind the Biggest IPO in Human History
item.description: SpaceX filed its S-1 for the largest IPO in history under ticker SPCX, targeting a $1.75-2 trillion valuation. But the filings reveal $29.1B in debt, $4.9B net loss last year, and a stunning $7.72B quarterly spend on AI infrastructure through the xAI merger. Starlink generates over $3.25B in quarterly revenue, but that cash is being funneled into the AI arms race against OpenAI. The technology is historic, but the financial math is as risky as it gets.

item.title: Palantir's AIP Is the Government AI Engine — Here's the Bull Case for $PLTR
item.description: Palantir's AIP platform is driving a new wave of government AI adoption, with TITAN, Maven, and other defense contracts pushing revenue toward $4B. Combined with accelerating commercial growth and improving margins, PLTR presents a compelling AI investment.

item.title: Nvidia's Blackwell Ramp: Inside the $100B AI Infrastructure Super-Cycle
item.description: Nvidia's Blackwell B200 is shipping in volume, hyperscaler orders are accelerating, and data center revenue is tracking toward $100B+. But with AMD's MI350 on the horizon and custom ASICs gaining traction, the bull case faces its most credible challenge yet.

item.title: Bill Gates Has Sold $20B+ of Microsoft Stock. Should You Follow?
item.description: Bill Gates has sold billions in Microsoft stock over the past five years, leading some retail investors to question their positions. But a deeper look reveals the sales are about diversification and philanthropy — not concern about Microsoft's AI-driven growth trajectory.

item.title: Redwire's Contract Runway: Why This $14 Stock Could Be the Next Space Rocket
item.description: Redwire has quietly built a $500M+ contract backlog across space manufacturing, solar arrays, and 3D printing. With revenue growing 30%+ annually and expanding roles in NASA's Artemis program, this small-cap space stock has the same setup Rocket Lab had at $5.

item.title: CrowdStrike vs Palo Alto Networks: Two Cybersecurity Giants, One Winner?
item.description: CrowdStrike and Palo Alto Networks have both surged in 2026, but their paths to growth are diverging. This head-to-head analysis compares revenue growth, margins, free cash flow, platform approach (Falcon vs Prisma/Strata), TAM expansion in XDR and SASE, and valuation to determine which cybersecurity giant offers the better risk-reward today.

item.title: Google I/O 2026: 5 Takeaways That Reshape the AI Landscape
item.description: Google I/O 2026 was a declaration that the AI wars have entered their platform phase. Gemini 3.0 closes the gap with GPT-5, AI Search monetizes at 15-20% higher CPMs, and TPU v6 gives Google a structural cost advantage competitors can't match.

item.title: Cerebras Landed 2026's Biggest IPO. Here's Why Nvidia Isn't Losing Sleep.
item.description: Cerebras Systems debuted on Nasdaq with a $26.6B valuation and surged 68% on its first day. The company's wafer-scale chips excel at AI inference but face a narrowing window as Nvidia's Blackwell architecture closes the gap. Here's what the IPO means for investors in both stocks.
